question: Can an immutable-baseline cumulative replay guard preserve the public replay ceiling while retaining the Q2.3 quantity acquisition path?
independent_variable: transactional AdamW with an all-slice cumulative functional replay guard

lineage.diagnosis: Q2.3's rotating local delta guard accepted all 200 attempts while the public balanced replay regression reached 0.026854690374003717. The local delta is not cumulative authority.

guard.baseline: arithmetic mean of the six frozen replay validation-window losses evaluated by the immutable ZERO.3 initialization
guard.candidate: arithmetic mean of those same six losses after the candidate AdamW update and before commit
guard.decision: reject non-finite candidates and candidates whose (candidate_mean - baseline_mean) / baseline_mean exceeds 0.015
guard.threshold_rationale: The hard limit reserves 25% of the frozen 0.02 public replay allowance for fixed-window-to-balanced-evaluation mismatch. It is chosen before Q2.4 training; the reconstructed Q2.3 trace is diagnostic only and cannot change it.

outcomes.go: a public checkpoint satisfies every quantity gate and replay regression <= 0.02, then the sealed promotion split also satisfies every quantity gate
outcomes.no_go: no jointly feasible public checkpoint exists before a frozen stop condition, with promotion remaining sealed

stop_conditions: replay exceeds 0.02 on two consecutive full evaluations
eight consecutive cumulative guard rejections end the active phase
no Pareto improvement for 200 committed updates
the frozen 1000 acquisition plus 400 consolidation attempt budget is exhausted
promotion_policy: open the disjoint promotion split exactly once only after a jointly feasible public checkpoint; otherwise record no-go
replication_policy: run seeds 1 and 3 only if diagnostic seed 2 passes
